title: Type Inference
description: Let TypeScript do the typing for you.

slides.title: TypeScript is Smart 🧠
slides.content:
You've seen explicit type annotations like `let size: number = 8;`.

However, writing annotations for everything can become tedious. Fortunately, you don't always have to!

TypeScript is incredibly good at **Type Inference**.

slides.title: Inference in Action
slides.content: If you initialize a variable and assign a value *on the same line*, TypeScript automatically infers the type.

quizData.question: If you type `let score = 10;` without an annotation, what does TypeScript do?
quizData.explanation: Because you assigned the value `10` immediately, TS infers the type as number.

slides.title: When Inference Fails 🛑
slides.content:
Inference only works if you provide an initial value right away.

If you simply declare a variable without a value, TS has nothing to base its inference on, so it dangerously defaults to `any`.

slides.title: The Golden Rule
slides.content: Follow this best practice for variables:
